common.tagline: Form your Florida business in minutes.

footer.tagline: The transparent, modern way to form a Florida business. Free Year-1 Registered Agent included.
footer.copyright: © {year} IncServices, Inc. · IncServices is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice.

comingSoon.heroSubhead: We launched in Florida with same-business-day filings, free Year-1 Registered Agent, and no surprise checkout fees. {state} is next on the list — join the early-access list and we'll email you the moment it opens.
comingSoon.ctaSecondary: I want to form a Florida company instead
comingSoon.trustEarlyAccess: Early-access pricing for the first launch wave
comingSoon.trustEmailOnly: We'll only email you about {state} availability
comingSoon.whySubhead: We'll bring the same playbook that worked for Florida — same-business-day filings, free Year-1 Registered Agent, and one all-in price — to {state} entrepreneurs.
comingSoon.whyItem1Body: We'll prepare and submit your {state} formation the same business day you sign — not 7-10 days later.
comingSoon.whyItem2Body: We'll provide a {state} physical address for service of process so your home address stays off the public record.
comingSoon.whyItem3Body: Your {state} state filing fee will already be included — no surprise checkout fees, no fake "premium" tiers.
comingSoon.floridaPathBody: We can file a Florida company for you today — most owners use a Florida LLC for asset protection or because they already do business there. No Florida residency required.

waitlist.title: Get early access to {state}
waitlist.subtitle: Drop your email and we'll let you know the moment {state} formations are open.
waitlist.successBody: We'll email {email} the moment {state} formations open. No spam in the meantime.
waitlist.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
waitlist.errorEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
waitlist.errorState: We couldn't determine your state. Try refreshing the page.
waitlist.errorAlready: You're already on the {state} early-access list. We'll be in touch.

hero.subhead: File your LLC or Corporation with the State of Florida — without the LegalZoom upsells, hidden fees, or 45-minute forms. We include a year of registered agent service, free.
hero.previewQuestion: What's the name of your business?
hero.previewSubtitle: We'll check availability against the Florida Sunbiz database in real time.
hero.previewSuccess: Great choice. No conflicts on the Florida Department of State record.
hero.previewNoSubscription: One clear price. No subscription. No surprise checkout fees.

features.subhead: Everything you need to form, fund, and run a Florida business — without the legal-tech overwhelm.
features.filed1DayBody: We submit to the Florida Department of State the same day you complete checkout, not 7–10 days later.
features.liveCheckBody: Real-time check against the Sunbiz database. We tell you if it's distinguishable on the record before you commit.
features.freeRABody: We maintain a Florida physical address for service of process — keeping your home address private.
features.documentsBody: Articles of Organization, Operating Agreement, EIN letter — formatted for banks, lenders, and the IRS.
features.complianceBody: Annual report reminders before the May 1 deadline. Optional managed filing means you'll never pay the $400 late fee.
features.transparentBody: One clear price per package — Florida filing fee already included. No hidden subscriptions. No surprise checkout fees.

howItWorks.step1Body: Choose LLC or Corporation, enter your name, and we check Sunbiz availability live.
howItWorks.step2Body: Basic Filing ($155), Bank-Ready Filing ($299), or Launch Concierge ($499). Florida filing fee already included.
howItWorks.step3Body: Type-to-sign your filing. One all-in price — no surprise checkout fees.
howItWorks.step4Body: Submitted same business day. Documents are emailed and stored in your dashboard.

pricing.headline: Pick a package. Pay once. Done.
pricing.subhead: One clear price per package — Florida filing fee already included. No subscriptions, no surprise checkout fees.
pricing.footnote: Each package includes the required Florida Department of State filing fee ($125 LLC / $70 Corporation). IncServices remits the state fee on your behalf.
pricing.bestFor_BASIC: I only need the legal filing
pricing.bestFor_STANDARD: Best for opening a bank account
pricing.tier_BASIC_desc: The legal filing, prepared and submitted by Florida specialists.
pricing.tier_STANDARD_desc: Everything banks ask for at account opening — handled.
pricing.tier_PREMIUM_desc: Bank-Ready plus year-round compliance, banking, and branding.
pricing.feat_articles: Articles of Organization / Incorporation prepared & submitted

comparison.subhead: We did the homework so you don't have to. Pricing reflects standard formation tiers as of 2026.

testimonials.subhead: From solopreneurs to real-estate syndicators — over 2,400 Floridians filed with us this year.

faq.headline: Questions, answered.
faq.respondWithin: and we'll get back to you within one business day.

ctaBanner.subhead: Most owners finish in 15 minutes. We submit to the state the same business day.
ctaBanner.footnote: No credit card required to start · Cancel any time

auth.passwordHint: At least 8 chars, one capital, one number
auth.termsAgreement: I agree that IncServices is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. I'm authorized to provide the information for forming a business.
auth.termsFooter: By creating an account, you agree to our
auth.sidePanelHeadline: Your Florida business, formed in fifteen minutes.
auth.sidePanelBody: Filing should feel as breezy as a Florida summer. We've reduced the typical 45-minute process to a clean 12-step flow — with transparent pricing.
auth.sidePanelSocial: Joined by 2,400+ Florida entrepreneurs this year.
auth.errorAccountExists: An account with that email already exists. Try signing in instead.

dashboard.title: Your business hub
dashboard.emptyBody: Form your first Florida business in under 15 minutes — we'll guide you through every step.
dashboard.archiveConfirm: Remove this draft from your dashboard? You can always start a new one. (Our team keeps a record so we can help if you reach out.)
dashboard.archiveError: We couldn't remove that draft. Please try again.
dashboard.resumeEyebrow: Pick up where you left off
dashboard.resumeUrgency: Finish today and we submit to Florida the same business day.
dashboard.documentsSubtitle: Download Articles, Operating Agreements, receipts, and other filings.
dashboard.documentsEmpty: Documents appear here once a filing is paid and submitted.
dashboard.complianceSubtitle: Florida requires annual reports between January 1 and May 1 each year. Miss the deadline and the state charges a non-waivable $400 late fee.
dashboard.complianceEmpty: Compliance deadlines appear here once you have an approved filing.
dashboard.complianceTip1: Annual reports due between January 1 and May 1 each year (11:59 PM ET)
dashboard.complianceTip3: $400 late fee is non-waivable — there is no hardship exception
dashboard.complianceTip4: Failure to file by the third Friday in September results in administrative dissolution
dashboard.complianceTip5: Reinstatement costs $100 (LLC) or $600 (Corp) plus all back fees

filingDetail.submittedToState: Filing submitted to Florida Department of State
filingDetail.annualReportEstimate: Once approved, due Jan 1 – May 1 next year

wizard.step1Subtitle: We'll handle the paperwork — you choose the structure.
wizard.step2Subtitle: We'll check the Florida Sunbiz database in real time.
wizard.step3Subtitle: One all-in price per package — Florida filing fee already included.
wizard.step4Subtitle: The physical or mailing location of your business operations.
wizard.step5Subtitle: Where you want official mail sent — can be the same as principal.
wizard.step6Subtitle: Required by Florida law. We can be yours, free for a year.
wizard.step7Subtitle: Who's authorized to act on behalf of the company?
wizard.step8Subtitle: Effective date, share count (Corp), professional purpose if applicable.
wizard.step9Subtitle: Confirm everything looks right and sign electronically.
wizard.step10Subtitle: Compliance, banking, branding — included or à la carte.
wizard.filingFeeIncludedNote: Florida filing fee included in package
wizard.freeRABanner: Year-1 Registered Agent is included free.
wizard.entityLLCDesc4: Florida filing fee included in package
wizard.entityCorpDesc4: Florida filing fee included in package
wizard.entityHint: 💡 Not sure which to choose? Most small business owners start with an LLC. You can elect S-Corp tax treatment later for tax savings as your business grows.
wizard.suffixWarnLLC: Florida LLC names must end with LLC, L.L.C., or Limited Liability Company.
wizard.suffixWarnCorp: Florida corporation names must end with Corp, Corporation, Inc, Incorporated, Co, or Company.
wizard.suffixDuplicateWarn: Don't include the legal ending here — it's already added by the dropdown.
wizard.nameStatusAvailable: No conflicts found in the Florida Sunbiz registry. A final state check will run at filing.
wizard.nameStatusExactConflict: A Florida entity with this name is already on the record. Florida requires names "distinguishable upon the record" — try a more distinct name.
wizard.nameStatusSimilar: We found similar Florida filings (some are inactive or cross-references and may still be held). Florida requires names "distinguishable upon the record" — try a more distinct variation.
wizard.nameStatusEnterMin: Enter at least 2 characters to check availability.
wizard.nameStatusError: We couldn't reach the Florida Sunbiz registry right now. Try again in a moment.
wizard.nameLockUrgency: Florida won't reserve this name until your formation is filed. Lock it in by continuing now — we submit the same business day.
wizard.nameTipsTitle: Quick tips for a strong name
wizard.nameTip1: Make it distinctive on the Florida record (not just punctuation differences)
wizard.nameTip2: Keep it under 70 characters for easier marketing
wizard.nameTip3: Check the matching .com domain availability — you can buy one in the next steps
wizard.nameTip4: Avoid restricted words (Bank, Trust, Insurance) without regulatory approval
wizard.principalTipBody: This is the address where you primarily conduct business. It can be a Florida address or out-of-state — Florida only requires the registered agent's address to be in Florida.
wizard.sameAsPrincipal: Use the same address as my principal office
wizard.mailingAddressNote: Mailing addresses can be a P.O. Box. Government correspondence will be sent here.
wizard.useOurAgentP4: Keeps your home address off the public record
wizard.useOwnAgentP1: Must have a Florida physical address (no P.O. Box)
wizard.useOwnAgentP2: Must consent to acting as agent
wizard.useOwnAgentP4: You'll need to update Florida if they change
wizard.poBoxRejection: P.O. Boxes are not allowed for registered agents — Florida law requires a physical street address.
wizard.raSignatureNote: By typing the agent's name below, you confirm they accept the appointment as Registered Agent and agree to act in this capacity. This is a legally binding electronic signature.
wizard.raSignatureLabel: Type the agent's name to sign
wizard.title: Title
wizard.ownershipWarn: ⚠ Ownership percentages add up to {total}%. Make sure they total 100% for clean tax filing.
wizard.correspondenceEmailHint: The Florida Department of State will send notices, annual report reminders, and confirmation emails to this address.
wizard.phoneHint: Useful for urgent compliance alerts. We never share your phone with third parties.
wizard.effectiveDateHint: Florida lets you backdate up to 5 days or schedule up to 90 days in the future. Leave blank to use today's date.
wizard.effectiveDateTip: 💡 Tip: If forming Oct 1 – Dec 31, set the effective date to January 1 of next year — you'll skip a full year of annual reports.
wizard.authorizedSharesHint: The number of shares your corporation is authorized to issue. Most early-stage companies start with 1,000–10,000,000.
wizard.businessPurposeHint: A brief description of what your business will do. Most LLCs leave this blank or write "any lawful business activity."
wizard.finalReviewBody: Once submitted, the Florida Department of State cannot easily amend your filing — you'd need to file a separate amendment. Take a moment to verify everything below.
wizard.signNote: Type your full name as the authorized representative. By doing so, you affirm the information is true and accurate, and your typed name has the same legal effect as a handwritten signature.
wizard.confirmAccurate: I confirm that the information above is true, complete, and accurate to the best of my knowledge. I understand IncServices is not a law firm and provides this service as a self-help tool.
wizard.packageDisclosure: Includes the required Florida filing fee. IncServices remits the state fee on your behalf.
wizard.afterPaymentStep1: We submit your filing to the Florida Department of State (same business day).
wizard.afterPaymentStep2: Florida reviews and approves your formation (typically 1–2 business days).
wizard.afterPaymentStep3: Approved Articles, EIN letter, and Operating Agreement appear in your dashboard.
wizard.afterPaymentStep4: We remind you 60 / 30 / 3 days before your annual report deadline so you never pay the $400 late fee.
wizard.errorCardDeclined: Your card was declined. Try a different card.
wizard.errorMembersRequired: Please add at least one member or officer with a name.
wizard.tierUpsellBody: The add-ons you've selected are bundled into the upgrade tier at a lower price. We'll keep your selections — you'll just pay less.
wizard.addOnsRecommendedHeadline: Banks and the IRS typically ask for these.
wizard.addOnsRecommendedBody: Most owners add these now so the bank account opens on the first try.
wizard.addOnsBannerEinReason: Banks ask for an EIN at account opening — we'll handle the IRS paperwork.
wizard.addOnsBannerOaReason: Multi-member LLCs need an Operating Agreement to satisfy banks and lenders.
wizard.confirmNotAvailable: This name may not be distinguishable on the Florida record. Continuing means the state may reject your filing. Use the suggested alternatives?
wizard.managementQuestion: How will this LLC be managed?
wizard.memberManagedDesc: The owners (members) run day-to-day operations. Most common for small LLCs.
wizard.managerManagedDesc: One or more designated managers run operations. Members are passive investors.
wizard.managerRequired: Manager-managed LLCs require at least one Manager.
wizard.ownershipTotalsBlock: ⚠ Ownership percentages add up to {total}%. They must total 100% before continuing.
wizard.oaUpsellLine: Ownership percentages are collected only when an Operating Agreement is included with your plan. You can add an Operating Agreement on the add-ons step.
wizard.oaMultiUpsellBody: Florida law strongly recommends multi-member LLCs document ownership splits, voting, and profit distribution. Banks usually require it to open a business account.
wizard.accountSettingsBlurb: We use the email on your account for state notices and document delivery. You can update it from <link>account settings</link>.
wizard.signNoteFlorida: Type your full name as the authorized representative. Per Florida Statutes, your typed name has the same legal effect as a handwritten signature on this filing.
wizard.titleHelpAMBRBody: A person listed as a member of the LLC who is authorized to sign filings, contracts, and bank documents on the company's behalf. Most member-managed LLCs use this title.
wizard.titleHelpMGRBody: A person designated to run day-to-day operations of a manager-managed LLC. Managers may or may not be members; their authority comes from the operating agreement.
wizard.titleHelpMGRMBody: Both a member and a manager — owns part of the LLC and runs operations. Common for small LLCs whose owners want to remain hands-on.
wizard.titleHelpAPBody: A non-member authorized to sign the Articles on the LLC's behalf (typically the organizer or attorney). Florida requires at least one authorized person to execute the filing.
wizard.titleHelpOfficerBody: Day-to-day leadership of the corporation — President, Vice-President, Secretary, Treasurer. Florida lets one person hold any or all officer titles.
wizard.titleHelpDirectorBody: Member of the board of directors — sets policy, oversees officers, and acts on behalf of the shareholders. Required by Chapter 607 (at least one).
wizard.titleHelpFooter: Florida only requires one signed authorized person/incorporator to file. Listing the full team here is optional but strongly recommended — banks and the IRS will ask for it on day one.
wizard.raHelpBody: A Florida registered agent is the company or person who receives legal mail (lawsuits, state notices, tax letters) on behalf of your business. They must have a physical Florida street address and be available during business hours. Per Chapter 605/607, Florida Statutes, your business cannot serve as its own agent.
wizard.raInternalConsent: I authorize {agentName} to act as the Registered Agent for my Florida business. I understand that the agent will sign Article III on my Articles, accepting the obligations of the position under Chapter 605/607, Florida Statutes — I do not need to type a signature for the agent.
wizard.raExternalLegalCopy: The agent must consent to acting as your Registered Agent. By typing the agent's name below, you confirm that they are familiar with and accept the obligations of the position as provided for in Chapter 605/607, Florida Statutes. This is a legally binding electronic signature.
wizard.tierUpsellOA: Add an Operating Agreement (${price}) — required to capture ownership percentages.
wizard.pendingDocumentMessage: This document is still being issued by Florida or the IRS. We will email you the moment it lands.
wizard.addOnDesc_registered_agent: Year-1 free. Florida physical address provided, legal mail scanned, your home address kept off the public record.
wizard.addOnDesc_ein: IRS Form SS-4 filed for you — federal Tax ID delivered within 1 business day. Required to open a business bank account.
wizard.addOnDesc_operating_agreement_single: Florida-tailored agreement defining ownership, governance, and succession — required by most banks at account opening.
wizard.addOnDesc_operating_agreement_multi: Custom agreement covering profit allocation, voting rights, capital calls, transfer restrictions, and dispute resolution.
wizard.addOnDesc_domain_com: Secure your online identity. Includes WHOIS privacy and free DNS management.
wizard.addOnDesc_cert_status: We pay Florida the certificate fee, request the document, and email it to you the moment it lands.
wizard.addOnDesc_cert_copy: State-certified copy of your filed Articles. We pay the state fee and deliver the certified PDF for banks and lenders.
wizard.addOnDesc_annual_report_managed: We file your Florida annual report on time — every year. Avoid the $400 non-waivable late penalty automatically.
wizard.addOnDesc_s_corp_election: Tax classification change. Pre-fills shareholder consents and provides mail-in instructions to the IRS.
wizard.addOnDesc_compliance_alerts: Year-round deadline tracking — annual reports, license renewals, BOI reports, sales-tax filings.

checkoutSuccess.headline1: Your filing is on its way to
checkoutSuccess.subhead: We've submitted {name} to the Florida Department of State. You'll receive an email confirmation in moments.
checkoutSuccess.checkEmailBody: We sent a confirmation receipt and a copy of your Articles to {email}.
checkoutSuccess.stateProcessesBody: The Florida Department of State will review and approve your formation. We'll email you the moment your business is officially formed.
checkoutSuccess.documentsReadyBody: You can find your Articles, Operating Agreement, and Filing Receipt in your dashboard right now.
checkoutSuccess.openBankBody: Most banks accept your Articles + EIN + Operating Agreement to open an account. We've prepared all three for you.
checkoutSuccess.annualRemindersBody: We'll email you 60, 30, and 3 days before next year's deadline (Jan 1 – May 1) so you never pay the $400 late fee.
checkoutSuccess.boiTitle: File your federal Beneficial Ownership Report (BOI)
checkoutSuccess.boiBody: FinCEN requires almost every new LLC and Corporation to file a BOI report within 90 days of formation. Filing is free at fincen.gov — but missing the deadline carries fines up to $591/day.
checkoutSuccess.bankBody: Most banks accept your Articles + EIN + Operating Agreement to open an account. Bring your photo ID and the EIN confirmation letter.
checkoutSuccess.annualReminderOptIn: Email me before next year's report
checkoutSuccess.annualReminderOptInDesc: Free reminders sent 60, 30, and 3 days before the May 1 deadline to your account email.

admin.filingsCount: {count} total · use the advance button to simulate state processing.
admin.outboxSubtitle: Mock email log — every email "sent" by the system is recorded here for inspection.
admin.selectEmail: Select an email to preview its contents.
admin.stateApprovalForceSubmitDesc: This filing is still in draft. Force-submit moves it to SUBMITTED so it shows up in the processing queue.
admin.stateApprovalIntro: Once Florida emails the official filing number, paste it below and approve. The Articles will be regenerated with the FILED stamp and the customer notified.
admin.sunbizFilingNumberFormatBad: Doesn't match Sunbiz format yet. Expecting one letter followed by 11 digits.
admin.sunbizPrefixHint: Heads up: Florida usually starts {entity} numbers with {prefix}. Double-check before approving.
admin.approvedHeadline: Approved by Florida Department of State
admin.uploadStateDocsDesc: Drop in PDFs received from Florida (Cert of Status, Certified Copy) or the IRS (CP 575 / EIN Letter). Uploading flips the row out of "Pending" and makes it downloadable from the customer dashboard.
admin.uploadAlreadyDesc: Already uploaded — re-uploading replaces the existing file.
admin.uploadPendingDesc: Customer paid for this add-on; placeholder is showing as Pending.
admin.uploadOptionalDesc: Customer did not purchase this add-on. Upload anyway only if Florida or the IRS issued one.
admin.filingNumberRequired: A Sunbiz filing number is required to approve this filing.
admin.filingNumberFormatBad: Filing number does not match the Sunbiz format (expected one letter followed by 11 digits, e.g. L26000123456).
